title: sorbus aucuparia subsp. maderensis
intro: Sorbus aucuparia subsp. maderensis, also known as the Madeiran whitebeam, is a subspecies of the mountain ash plant, native to the island of Madeira. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Sorbus aucuparia subsp. maderensis include its ability to soothe stomach problems, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or tinctures made from its leaves and bark, which can be used to create teas, salves, or other remedies.
